subject: fix(visual): modal h3 type-scale parity + opacity-scale outliers
body:
Low-severity visual consistency drift from the frontend.visual sweep.
Filtered the agent's six findings down to two real ones; dropped
false positives (tag-badge context split, pagination gap drift,
chip button sizing — all legitimate by-context differences).

1. Modal heading drift.
   TransactionEditModal's h3 used `text-base font-semibold`; every
   other modal (ConfirmDialog, TutorialModal, InstallInstructionsModal,
   HelpDrawer) uses `text-lg font-semibold`. Bumped both TransactionEditModal
   sites (details view + edit mode) to `text-lg` for parity.

2. Opacity-scale outliers.
   App's `text-base-content/*` distribution: 42 at /60, 30 at /40,
   26 at /70, 11 at /80, 4 at /50, 1 at /20. The four /50 uses were
   one-offs that don't fit the scale: ClassificationBadge "Once-off"
   text, CashflowGraph History/Forecast section labels, BurgerMenu
   menu-trigger icon. Normalised all four to /60. Contrast marginally
   improves as a side effect.

Type-check clean. 116/116 tests pass. No /50 opacity usages remain
in .vue files. Production chunks unchanged in size (CSS-only edits).

Tags: visual, consistency, type-scale, opacity, daisyui
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 1
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: ux-polish
Semver: patch
title: modal h3 type-scale parity + opacity-scale outliers
